title: doryopteris decipiens
intro: Doryopteris decipiens, also known as the Decipiens Palm Vetch, is a type of climbing fern native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Doryopteris decipiens is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal preparations, such as salves and ointments, for topical application.
